title: 10 best herbal tinctures for puffy eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The aloe vera extract in the tincture reduces swelling and redness caused by fatigue, allergies, or fluid retention, leaving the skin looking smoother and more radiant. Additionally, the soothing effects of the aloe help to calm irritated eye tissues, reducing puffiness and dark circles. Regular use of herbal aloe tinctures can lead to a refreshed and revitalized appearance, making it an effective natural remedy for combating puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe tinctures contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation under the eyes, which can contribute to puffiness.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from free radical damage, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness.

Cooling and soothing properties: Herbal aloe tinctures have a cooling effect when applied topically, which can help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling and redness under the eyes, providing instant relief for puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and swelling, making it effective in alleviating puffy eyes.
Aloe-β-sitosterol: Aloe-β-sitosterol, a type of phytosterol, possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Aloe-cinnamic acid: Aloe-cinnamic acid, a phenolic acid, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help to minimize puffiness and promote healthy skin around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce puffiness and swelling.
Stems: Some tinctures also use the stems, as they contain a similar balance of anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, helping to alleviate eye puffiness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from mature plants for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the aloe vera leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Chop the dried aloe vera leaves into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a 1 to 5 ratio.
Store the aloe vera vodka mixture in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the aloe vera tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint has a cooling effect that constricts blood vessels and reduces swelling, instantly relaxing and de-puffing the under-eye area. Additionally, peppermint's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and reduce puffiness caused by environmental stressors. When applied topically, herbal peppermint tinctures provide a refreshing and soothing relief for puffy eyes, leaving skin feeling smooth and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and redness around the eyes, alleviating puffiness and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in peppermint tincture protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Peppermint's essential oils help constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps to reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and improving circulation, which in turn reduces swelling.
Rosmarin酸 (Rosmarinic acid): A phenolic compound that possesses anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce puffiness and inflammation by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals.
Linalool: A terpene with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing puffiness and promoting a more even blood flow.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Stems: Rich in menthol, which can help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling in the eyes.
Leaves: Also rich in menthol and other compounds that can help cool and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture.
Grind 100 grams of dried mentha x piperita leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Steep the powder in 500 milliliters of vodka at a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts solvent for 4 to 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. The tincture's potent antioxidants also work to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to puffiness and discoloration. Additionally, the tincture's hydrating properties help to lock in moisture, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles around the delicate eye area. As a result, Kansas coneflower tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for reducing puffy eyes and leaving skin looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like triterpenoids, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, making it effective in reducing puffiness.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Kansas Coneflower tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to eye puffiness and dark circles.
Vasodilation and blood flow promotion: The tincture's vasodilatory properties can help improve blood circulation around the eyes, reducing swelling and puffiness caused by poor blood flow.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce puffiness by reducing inflammation and promoting the breakdown of excess fluid in the body.
Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These plant-based antioxidants help alleviate puffy eyes by reducing inflammation and fighting off oxidative stress in the skin.

Alkylamides (specifically, cis- and trans-11,12-dihydroechinacin): These compounds help alleviate puffy eyes by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties and promoting the reduction of swelling in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of compounds like alkylamides and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce puffiness and swelling.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their ability to calm and soothe the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of clean dried echinacea angustifolia root in the early morning.
Chop the dried echinacea root into small pieces to increase its surface area.
Combine the chopped echinacea root with 500 milliliters of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle after four weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with puffy eyes because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce swelling and congestion. The tannins present in the leaves of the maidenhair tree have a natural vasoconstrictive effect, which helps to minimize the appearance of dark circles and puffiness. Additionally, the antioxidants and flavonoids found in the tincture help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing redness and irritation. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from puffy eyes, leaving you with a refreshed and revitalized appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to puffiness and swelling in the eye area.

Vasodilatory properties: The tincture's active compounds, particularly ginkgolic acid, cause blood vessels to dilate, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation around the eyes by increasing blood flow and oxygenation to the affected area.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as salicylic acid and terpenes, can help reduce inflammation and redness associated with puffy eyes, making it an effective natural remedy for this condition.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can reduce puffiness and promote a more even appearance under the eyes.
Bilobalide: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate swelling and puffiness associated with eye problems.
Quercetin: As a powerful antioxidant, quercetin can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which may contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are used to make tinctures for puffy eyes due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make tinctures for puffy eyes due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation.

Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make tinctures for puffy eyes due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which can help reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves or seeds at the right time of day.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 1-2 hours or use a dehydrator.
Combine 1 part ginkgo biloba material with 2 parts vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains calendulic acid, a powerful natural compound that reduces swelling and soothes irritated skin around the eyes. When applied topically or taken internally as a tincture, it quickly absorbs into the skin to combat puffiness, dark circles, and fine lines. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals, further reducing inflammation and promoting healthy, radiant-looking under-eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Pot Marigold's antioxidants and flavonoids reduce puffiness by neutralizing free radicals and soothing inflamed tissues, thus reducing swelling and discoloration.
Vasodilatory effects: The tannins and flavonoids in Pot Marigold help to relax blood vessels, which reduces inflammation and congestion, thereby decreasing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Anti-edema properties: Pot Marigold's saponins have been shown to inhibit the production of bradykinin, a peptide that causes fluid retention and swelling, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing eye puffiness.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These pigments help reduce puffiness by acting as powerful antioxidants that fight inflammation and protect delicate skin around the eyes.
Sesquiterpenes (Calendulic acid): These compounds help reduce puffiness by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate swelling and redness associated with puffy eyes.

Flavonoids (Quercetin): These plant-based compounds help reduce puffiness by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect skin from damage caused by puffiness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part for making calendula tinctures, as they contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce puffiness and soothe irritated skin.
Leaves: The leaves of Calendula officinalis also contain anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate puffy eyes.

Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis, although less commonly used than flowers, may also be used to make tinctures for puffy eyes, as they possess some anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days to prevent mold.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow extraction to occur.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease congestion. The curcuminoids in turmeric inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, allowing the skin to recover from fatigue and fluid retention. As a natural decongestant, it also reduces puffiness caused by sinus pressure and allergies. Additionally, its antioxidant effects help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors, leaving them looking bright and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and puffiness in the eyes.
Antioxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin around the eyes, reducing puffy appearance.
Vasodilatory: The bioactive compounds in turmeric tinctures, including curcumin, may help dilate blood vessels, which can reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes and alleviate puffiness.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce puffiness and inflammation in the eyes.
Demethoxycurcumin: A demethylated form of curcumin, this compound has also been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate puffiness and eye swelling.

Furanocoumarin: While the exact mechanism is not fully understood, furanocoumarins present in Curcuma longa have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects, which can help reduce puffiness and improve blood circulation around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Anti-inflammatory properties - Curcuma longa rhyzomes are used in tinctures to combat puffy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort.

Roots: Antioxidant properties - Curcuma longa roots are used in tinctures to combat puffy eyes due to their antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage and promote overall eye health.
Rhyzomes: Anti-edemic properties - Curcuma longa rhyzomes are used in tinctures to combat puffy eyes due to their anti-edemic properties, which help reduce fluid accumulation and alleviate puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100g of dried curcuma longa rhizomes and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and combine them with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container.
Discard the solids and store the liquid tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb borage contains a rich source of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an essential fatty acid that has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling. When applied topically as a tincture, GLA in borage works to constrict blood vessels and calm the skin around the eyes, effectively reducing puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help protect the delicate eye area from environmental stressors and damage.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Borage tincture's anti-inflammatory effects, thanks to its rich omega-6 fatty acid content, help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in borage tincture, such as beta-carotene and lutein, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye puffiness and dark circles.
Vasoconstriction relief: Borage tincture's ability to relax blood vessels helps alleviate the appearance of puffy eyes by reducing fluid buildup and relieving constriction around the orbital bone.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These natural glycosides have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness around the eyes.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce eye puffiness caused by inflammation and oxidative stress.
Squalene: This triterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its ability to reduce eye puffiness and promote overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for puffy eyes because they are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make tinctures for puffy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and swelling.

Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures for puffy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help reduce swelling, redness, and itching caused by allergies or irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency usually in morning hours.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped material with 500ml of 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and alleviate discoloration. The tincture's active compounds, including parthenolide and sesquiterpenes, work to constrict blood vessels and minimize fluid retention, resulting in a noticeable reduction in puffiness. Additionally, feverfew's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving it looking smoother and more radiant. With regular use, herbal feverfew tinctures can provide long-term relief from puffy eyes and leave you with a refreshed, rejuvenated appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew contains various anti-inflammatory compounds, such as parthenolide and sesquiterpenes, which help reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and molecules.

Vasodilatory activity: The flavonoids present in feverfew tinctures can dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing fluid accumulation under the eyes, thereby helping to alleviate puffiness.

Antioxidant properties: Feverfew's antioxidant compounds help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffy eye appearance.
remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: A sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Cichoriin: A sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties that may help alleviate eye puffiness by relaxing facial muscles and reducing fluid retention.
Apigenin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that may help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes by blocking the action of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used in tinctures due to their high concentration of feverfew's anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling.
Flowers: The flowers are used in tinctures for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate sinus pressure and reduce puffiness.
Stems: The stems are used in tinctures for their ability to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which can cause inflammation and swelling in the eyes, leading to puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves on a dry sunny morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days at room temperature.
Combine 1 part of dried tanacetum parthenium with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease tension. When used topically as an eye cream or added to a compress, the soothing essence of lavender calms irritated tissues and relaxes the orbicularis oculi muscle, diminishing puffiness and dark circles. remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with puffy eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which in turn reduces swelling and puffiness.

Stems: They are used due to their ability to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation, making them a useful addition to eye creams and tinctures for puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers by cutting stems near the base of the plants during peak bloom.
Combine 1 part of the dried flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.

Matricaria chamomilla terpenoids (including alpha-bisabolol and other terpenes): These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties, which can help alleviate puffiness by reducing inflammation and soothing the skin around the eyes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak bloom when the plant is in full sun.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 35°c for 2 hours to preserve their active compounds.
Combine the dried flowers with 500ml of 25% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow extraction to occur.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with puffy eyes because its unique properties provide a natural and effective solution for reducing puffiness and dark circles. The tincture's active ingredients, including eucalyptol and cineole, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to constrict blood vessels, reduce swelling, and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves for optimal potency and flavor.
Weigh out 50 grams of eucalyptus globulus leaves and add them to a clean glass jar.
Pour 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka over the eucalyptus leaves, ensuring they are completely submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the tincture to infuse.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness. Vasodilatory properties: Ginger's vasodilatory properties can help dilate blood vessels, allowing for better circulation and removal of excess fluid and toxins from the eye area, thereby reducing puffiness.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale roots from your garden or purchase them from a trusted supplier.
Wash the roots thoroughly with pure water to remove any dirt debris or contaminants.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Fill a clean glass jar with 80-proof vodka and add 1 ounce of chopped ginger per pint of liquid let it steep for.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discard the solids and store the tincture in.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers for at least 25 percent of the total volume.
Clean and dry the plant material to prevent contamination by handling with clean gloves.
Combine one part of plant material with two parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking the jar daily to extract constituents.
Strain and filter the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding any solids and transfer to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation. The bioactive compounds present in hawthorn, such as flavonoids and saponins, work together to constrict blood vessels and decrease swelling. This natural process helps to alleviate the appearance of puffy eyes, leaving you with a smoother, more radiant complexion. Antioxidant properties: The rich antioxidant content in hawthorn tinctures protects the eyes from oxidative stress, reducing damage to ocular tissues and promoting overall eye health.

Vasodilatory properties: Hawthorn's flavonoids also help relax blood vessels, which can constrict and contribute to puffy eyes; by dilating them, hawthorn reduces swelling and promotes healthy circulation.

Fruits: Hawthorn fruits contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and discoloration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Weigh out one part of dried crataegus monogyna herbs by weight and combine with three parts high-proof vodka.
Transfer herb-vodka mixture to a clean glass jar and seal tightly for maceration at room temperature.
Allow mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking jar weekly to ensure even extraction, before straining through cheesecloth.
Filter tincture through activated charcoal or coffee filters to remove any remaining particulate matter and store in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in parsley, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the delicate skin around the eyes. Additionally, parsley's diuretic properties help to reduce water retention, further contributing to a more radiant and refreshed appearance. As a natural remedy, parsley tinctures offer a gentle yet effective solution for alleviating puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Parsley's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and puffiness under the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Parsley's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by puffy eyes.
Cicatrizant properties: Parsley's cicatrizant properties help stimulate collagen production and improve skin elasticity, which can reduce the appearance of dark circles and puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of parsley contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.
Buds: Parsley buds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petroselinum crispum leaves in the morning after dew has dried off.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or air drying method for 24 hours.
Measure out 500 grams of dried petroselinum crispum leaves and mix with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka.
Store the mixture in a dark glass jar and let it steep for 14 days, shaking daily at the same time.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb has been used for centuries to treat various skin issues, including puffiness and swelling. When applied topically as a tincture, dandelion's active compounds work to reduce fluid retention and combat inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes. This results in a noticeable reduction in puffiness and dark circles, leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more radiant. Regular use of herbal dandelion tinctures can help maintain a youthful and refreshed appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce puffiness by minimizing swelling and redness around the eyes, providing relief from irritated or tired eyes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in dandelion tincture help combat free radicals that can cause eye strain and puffiness, promoting healthy and bright-eyed appearance.
Diuretic: Dandelion's diuretic property helps to reduce water retention and edema around the eyes, which can contribute to puffy eyes, by increasing urine production and flushing out excess fluids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain antioxidants and fatty acids that help reduce inflammation and promote healthy blood circulation, which can help alleviate puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves from a pesticide-free environment for optimal potency and quality.
Rinse the plant material with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry gently with paper towels.
Combine 200g of dried taraxacum officinale flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to amber glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The antispasmodic properties of sage also ease tension in the eye muscles, relaxing them and reducing puffiness. Additionally, sage's antioxidant compounds combat free radicals that can contribute to puffy eyes, leaving the area looking smoother and more radiant. By applying a few drops of sage tincture to the affected area, individuals can experience rapid relief from puffy eyes and enjoy a more refreshed, revitalized appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes by reducing inflammation and congestion.
Antioxidant: Sage's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to dark circles and puffiness under the eyes.
Constricting agent: Sage's tannins work as a constricting agent, helping to reduce blood vessel dilation and reduce fluid accumulation around the eyes, thereby reducing puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots help to reduce puffiness and soothe irritated skin around the eyes.
Leaves: Sage leaves contain flavonoids and terpenes that can help to constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and dark circles under the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves using a clean towel or paper towels.
Combine 250ml of vodka and 100g of dried salvia officinalis leaves in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in elder flowers, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease puffiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid retention. When applied topically as a tincture, these active ingredients can be absorbed quickly into the skin, providing fast relief from puffy eyes caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or poor circulation. As a result, elder tinctures offer a natural and effective solution for banishing bags and brightening up tired-looking peepers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Elderflower's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and puffiness by protecting the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors.
Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in elderflowers have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and alleviate congestion, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
Vasodilatory: Elderflower's ability to dilate blood vessels can help improve circulation and reduce fluid retention, which can be beneficial for alleviating puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and edema associated with puffy eyes.
Barks: Contain anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean sambucus nigra: collect fresh or dried flowers of sambucus nigra, ensuring they are free from contaminants and debris.
Prepare the plant material: weigh out 100g of sambucus nigra flowers and chop them into smaller pieces to increase their surface area.
Infuse the tincture: combine the prepared plant material with 400ml of high-proof ethanol (at least 95%) in a glass container, ensuring the plant material is completely submerged.

Steep and filter the tincture: allow the mixture to steep for 14 days in a cool, dark place, shaking the container daily; then strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.

Bottle and store the tincture: transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, storing them in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years or until the desired potency is reached.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease congestion. The herbal extract also improves blood circulation, allowing excess fluid to drain more efficiently and reducing puffiness. Additionally, ginseng's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals that can cause eye fatigue and irritation, promoting healthy, smooth skin around the eyes. This natural remedy provides a soothing and revitalizing solution for puffy eyes, leaving you with a brighter, more refreshed appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginseng tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like panaxadiol and panaxatriol, which can reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness in the eyes.
Vasodilatory properties: The vasodilating effects of ginseng help to dilate blood vessels, reducing pressure on the eyes and decreasing the appearance of puffiness.
Antioxidant properties: Ginseng tincture is rich in antioxidants like panaxquinone and ginsenosides, which can combat free radicals that contribute to eye inflammation and puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginseng plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of ginseng contain saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for soothing puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality panax ginseng roots in the fall season for maximum potency.
Cleanse and dry the roots thoroughly under sterile conditions within three days.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka or brandy at a ratio of one part herb to four parts solvent.
Allow the mixture to steep for six weeks in a cool dark place away from direct sunlight.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store it in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that soothes and calms irritated skin and reduces swelling. When applied topically to the eye area, lemon balm tincture can help to de-puff and refresh the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness. Additionally, its mild astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, further reducing inflammation and leaving the eyes looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tincture's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and puffiness around the eyes, reducing their appearance.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm tincture, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and redness caused by allergic reactions or irritants, making puffy eyes less noticeable.
Muscle-relaxing properties: Lemon balm's flavonoids and terpenes can help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and puffiness caused by fatigue, stress, or poor circulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and soothe puffy eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the lemon balm plant contain a high concentration of essential oils, including citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves or purchase dried material in bulk quantity.
Chop the fresh herbs into small pieces to ensure maximum extraction and combine with water.
Steep the herb mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow optimal release of bioactive compounds and essential oils.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove solids and achieve desired clarity.
Transfer the extracted tincture to dark glass bottles and store in cool dry place for at least two weeks.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for puffy eyes are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate eye puffiness caused by fluid retention, allergies, or fatigue. These tinctures help to reduce swelling and promote blood circulation around the eyes, providing relief from discomfort and visual impairment. The best herbal tinctures for puffy eyes include Euphrasia officinalis, Calendula officinalis, Hamamelis virginiana, Aloe barbadensis, Echinacea purpurea, Hypericum perforatum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Vitis vinifera, Aloe vera, and Vaccinium macrocarpon. Without treatment, puffy eyes can affect daily life, making it difficult to work, drive, or even maintain a social life due to self-consciousness and discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to puffy eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures are various skin conditions, such as eczema, acne, and rosacea. Herbal tinctures containing calendula, chamomile, and lavender have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin. These tinctures can also be used to address dark circles and under-eye puffiness by reducing inflammation and promoting circulation. Additionally, some herbal tinctures may help alleviate sinus pressure and congestion, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with puffy eyes is a blend of Calendula, Echinacea, and Eyebright. Calendula reduces inflammation and soothes the skin, while Echinacea boosts the immune system to combat underlying causes of puffiness. Eyebright, as its name suggests, specifically targets and reduces puffiness around the eyes. This combination can be taken sublingually or applied topically to the affected area with a carrier oil, providing quick relief and promoting overall eye health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture, made from the extract of the Euphrasia officinalis plant, is known to reduce swelling and alleviate eye strain. Its ability to soothe and calm the eyes also helps to minimize puffiness. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Euphrasia officinalis help to combat free radicals that can cause inflammation and lead to puffy eyes, providing a natural and effective solution for this common issue.
remedy_constituents: Leucosin: Acts as a vasoconstrictor, which helps to reduce swelling and alleviate puffy eyes by constricting blood vessels.
Phenylpropanoids: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with puffy eyes.
Saponins: Exhibits vasoconstrictive and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation.
Flowers: They are utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm puffy eyes.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to absorb and concentrate the plant's bioactive compounds, making them a valuable component in tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh aerial parts of euphrasia officinalis when they are in full bloom and have reached their peak potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dry place or using a dehydrator at 40°c for 8 hours.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for efficient solvent action.
Steep 100 grams of the chopped dried plant material in 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol for 14 days in a glass container.
Filter the tincture using a coffee filter or a buchner funnel to remove the plant residues from the liquid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The flavonoids and triterpenoids in Calendula work to reduce swelling and ease irritation, making it an effective remedy for puffy eyes. Its ability to calm and protect the delicate skin around the eyes also helps to reduce puffiness and promote a more rested appearance. Regular use of Calendula tinctures may also help to improve the overall health and appearance of the skin.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in puffy eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, Quercetin helps alleviate puffiness by reducing vascular permeability and preventing the release of histamine.

Sesquiterpenes: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate puffiness and reduce redness in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Leaves: They are used because of their ability to calm and cool the skin, providing relief from puffiness and discomfort.
Stems: They are used to create a tincture that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing around the eyes, making them less puffy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the calendula officinalis flowers in their full bloom stage when they are a vibrant yellow color.
Clean the flowers thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris by gently rinsing with distilled water.
Combine the cleaned flowers with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight and volume respectively.
Place the mixture in a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids and label the bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The tincture's high concentration of hamamelic acid and flavonoids helps to reduce swelling and constrict blood vessels, making it an effective remedy for alleviating puffiness. Additionally, its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin helps to reduce redness and discomfort, leaving the under-eye area feeling refreshed and rejuvenated. This makes it a popular natural solution for addressing puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: This compound has astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness in the eyes.
Gallic acid: As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, gallic acid helps combat inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to puffy eyes.
Rutoside: Rutoside, a flavonoid glycoside, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help minimize swelling and redness in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Hamamelis virginiana leaves are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes.
Buds: Hamamelis virginiana buds contain a high concentration of tannins, which have astringent properties that help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling around the eyes.
Roots: Hamamelis virginiana roots are a rich source of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help calm and soothe the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in the early morning after a gentle rain.
Chop the hamamelis virginiana plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area.
Combine the chopped hamamelis virginiana plant material with 500ml of 45% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and shake the container for 2-3 weeks every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties and high concentration of antioxidants. The tincture contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that help to reduce swelling and ease puffiness. Additionally, the tincture's hydrating and soothing effects help to calm and relax the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness. Regular use of aloe barbadensis tincture can lead to a more refreshed and revitalized look, making it a popular natural remedy for puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which contributes to puffy eyes.
Phenolic acids: These compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness.
Glycoproteins: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote the breakdown of excess fluids, which in turn reduces the appearance of puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe barbadensis contain compounds like glycoproteins, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of Aloe barbadensis are rich in antioxidants, which can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes.
Buds: The buds of Aloe barbadensis contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce puffiness and redness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe barbadensis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the leaves into small pieces using sharp scissors and a cutting board.
Combine the chopped leaves with 70% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture contains compounds like alkylamides and polyphenols that reduce swelling and inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes. These properties also help to combat allergies and sinus issues that can cause puffiness. Additionally, Echinacea's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, further contributing to puffy eyes. As a result, the tincture can help to reduce puffiness and leave the skin looking more refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_constituents: Alkylphenols: Help reduce puffiness by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that contribute to swelling.
Iridoid glycosides: Assist in reducing puffiness by modulating the immune response, decreasing inflammation, and improving circulation, which helps to reduce fluid retention.

Caffeic acid derivatives: Help alleviate puffiness by possessing antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which in turn can decrease inflammation and alleviate swelling.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness.
Roots: Utilized for their rich content of glycoproteins and polysaccharides, which have immunomodulatory effects and help alleviate puffiness and inflammation.
Leaves: Employed for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the skin around puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves when in full bloom to ensure potency and optimal flavor.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on a paper bag for 7 to 10 days.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase its surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 part dried echinacea purpurea with 2 parts vodka in a glass jar and seal it tightly for 4 to 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture, also known as St. John's Wort, contains hyperforin, a compound that reduces swelling and redness. It also has a cooling effect on the skin, which can help to reduce puffiness. Additionally, the tincture's ability to improve circulation can help to reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, leaving the skin looking smoother and more radiant. This natural remedy can provide a soothing and effective solution for puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help reduce inflammation and swelling, making them effective in alleviating puffy eyes.
Naphthodianthrones (e.g., Hyperforin and Hypericin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and reduce puffiness around the eyes.
Phenolic acids (e.g., Quercetin): They possess powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling and improve circulation, thus alleviating puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce puffiness.
Flowers: High in hyperforin and hypericin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm the eyes.
Roots: Contain bioflavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce swelling and puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a quantity of flowering tops of the plant with bright yellow petals and four petals.
Chop the plant material into smaller pieces to release its essential oils and bioactive compounds.
Combine the chopped plant material with a solvent like glycerin or ethanol at a ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and bottle the resulting tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease irritation. The active compound glycyrrhetinic acid has been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to combat the underlying causes of puffy eyes. By applying the tincture around the orbital area, individuals may experience a reduction in puffiness, resulting in a more rested and refreshed appearance. This natural remedy may provide a soothing solution for those seeking an alternative to harsh chemicals.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help reduce puffiness and inflammation by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that causes blood vessels to swell.
Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate puffiness and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, which can help reduce puffiness, swelling, and redness around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and swelling.
Leaves: The leaves are used in tinctures for their anti-inflammatory properties and to help alleviate puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in the early morning to capture optimal medicinal properties.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 70% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the root mixture in a dark glass container for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera tinctures helps with puffy eyes because it contains potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties found in the grape plant. The tannins and flavonoids present in Vitis vinifera help to reduce inflammation and swelling, soothing the delicate skin around the eyes. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing fluid retention, Vitis vinifera tinctures can effectively alleviate puffiness and leave the eyes looking refreshed and revitalized. This natural remedy can be a simple and effective solution for banishing puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: Helps reduce puffiness by acting as a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can alleviate swelling and protect the delicate skin around the eyes.

Resveratrol: Contributes to the reduction of puffiness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the breakdown of excess fluids, thereby alleviating swelling and discomfort.

Kaempferol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness by inhibiting the activation of inflammatory pathways and promoting the relaxation of blood vessels, which in turn reduces fluid accumulation around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits are used to make tinctures due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce puffiness and swelling.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make tinctures due to their cooling and soothing effects, which help calm and reduce puffiness.
Seeds: Seeds are used to make tinctures due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce puffiness and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vitis vinifera leaves and stems in early morning when the dew is still present for best results.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 1 part dried plant material with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe and calm the skin. The tannins and anthraquinones present in aloe vera tincture reduce swelling and inflammation, making it an effective remedy for puffy eyes. Additionally, aloe vera's hydrating properties help to replenish moisture and reduce puffiness, leaving the eyes feeling refreshed and rejuvenated. This natural remedy can be applied topically or taken internally to promote healthy eye health and reduce the appearance of puffy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Alginates: Help reduce puffiness by soothing and calming the skin, thereby reducing inflammation and swelling.
Glycoproteins: Assist in reducing fluid retention and puffiness by promoting the breakdown and removal of excess fluids from the skin.
Phenolic compounds: Exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate puffiness by reducing swelling and redness around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera are commonly used to make tinctures for puffy eyes due to their rich content of anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds.
Barks: Although not as commonly associated with Aloe vera, the barks of the plant are occasionally used for their potential anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Aloe vera are sometimes used to make tinctures, adding to their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves ideally in the morning when the plant is most potent for a maximum yield of 20 leaves per cup.
Chop the leaves into small pieces approximately 1-2 inches in size to increase their surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine the chopped aloe vera pieces with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by volume and mix well for uniformity.
Steep the mixture in a glass container with a lid in a cool dark place for a minimum of 2 weeks to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove any solids and store the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon tinctures helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease eye strain. The antioxidant-rich properties of cranberry (Vaccinium macrocarpon) tincture also help to neutralize free radicals, promoting healthy blood flow and reducing puffiness. Additionally, the tincture's ability to improve lymphatic circulation and promote drainage of excess fluids can help to depuff the eyes, leaving them looking refreshed and rejuvenated. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to any skincare routine.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and puffiness by neutralizing free radicals that cause swelling.
Ellagic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate puffiness by reducing the underlying causes of swelling.
Quercetin: This flavonoid acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing puffiness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that contribute to swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for puffy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: They are used to make tinctures for puffy eyes due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are used to make tinctures for puffy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 pounds of vaccinium macrocarpon in mid to late summer when ripe and dry.
Chop the harvested vaccinium macrocarpon into small pieces and dry them completely in a low oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Combine 1 cup of the dried vaccinium macrocarpon with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar for a ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dark place.
